nodes.comment: ONE RUNG ABOVE WHERE THE DOCUMENT PUTS IT, and this is the story's one stage divergence. The V2 tree lists Whamon as a Champion feeding MetalMamemon, but this asset pack files its only animated sheet under Perfect/Whamon.png, so the roster has it at Perfect and a node claiming Adult would resolve to no art at all. Rather than drop a named Digimon, it stands BESIDE MetalMamemon instead of beneath it, off the same two Champions the document puts on its row (Garurumon and Yukidarumon), and shares MetalMamemon's Mega - the invented arrow, needed because a terminal Perfect is a Digimon that can never evolve again. Pendulum Color V2 Deep Savers puts Whamon at Perfect too (Rukamon -> Whamon -> Plesiomon), so US-139 will need a line-scoped id for it.

nodes.comment: THE JUNK CHAMPION, and it is NOT from the source document: the Pendulum Color V1 section draws no junk branch at all, while every Child and Adult in this app needs an isDefault edge a neglected Digimon can reach by doing nothing (US-061, EvolutionCriteriaTests). The five Digital Monster Color trees each had one to borrow — Numemon, Vegimon, Scumon, Nanimon, Raremon — and this tree had to choose one. The document writes the row as "Mega: WarGreymon -> Ultra: Omegamon (Jogress)" - Omegamon is a RECIPE in jogress.json, not an edge, which is the treatment every other tree's Ultra row got, so this node is terminal. This tree is the second to hold BOTH of that recipe's parents after penc-nsp, and the first whose document draws the Jogress on both of them.
The top of Leomon's thread; `saberleomon` is penc-nsp's.

The top of Gabumon's thread, and the other parent of the Omegamon recipe. Its row reads "Mega: MetalGarurumon -> Ultra: Omegamon (Jogress)" for the same reason pencvb_wargreymon's does, so it is terminal too.
The top of Angemon's holy thread, and a plain roster id: no other line draws it.
The top of Tailmon's thread. A plain roster id.

The document's "Mega: Cherubimon (Virtue)", the top of Wizardmon's thread and the second climb out of holyangemon. The bracket is part of the name here rather than a respelling: the pack ships Cherubimon (Virtue) and Cherubimon (Vice) as two sheets, and this tree spends both - Virtue as the document's earned Mega and Vice as the junk one.
The top of the unlockable slot's thread. `Rasenmon_Fury.png` is a separate sheet and a separate roster entry; the document writes the name unqualified, so the unqualified sheet is the one wired.

The junk Mega - the top of the invented neglect chain, and the point of choosing that chain: a neglected Virus Buster ends as the Vice counterpart of the Virtue the document draws over Wizardmon. Named nowhere in the tree markdown; a test greps the bundled document to keep that true.

Agumon (2006) is Agumon under a second sheet, so by the sweep's variant rule its egg hangs off Agumon's own line rather than opening one. Wikimon draws Agumon (2006 Anime Version) from Koromon, which is exactly what `botamon` leads to here, so the egg needs no new rung to be honest about where it goes. Second egg on this line beside agu_digitama; a line may have several, and EggHatcher reads one edge per egg.

Gabumon Black's egg, placed by the variant rule alone: Wikimon records no separate baby thread for the Black variant, so no citation is claimed for one. It hatches onto the base form's spine, Punimon -> Tsunomon -> Gabumon, which is this line.
Elecmon's egg. Elecmon is already a Rookie of this line - `tsunomon` falls to it - so the egg belongs on the line that leads to it rather than opening a second Elecmon thread.
Kunemon's egg. Kunemon is already a Rookie of this line (`tokomon` -> `kunemon`), so its egg roots the line that reaches it.

Angoramon's egg. Angoramon is already a Rookie of this line (`pencnsp_koromon` -> `angoramon`), so its egg roots the line that reaches it.

Wikimon: Yuki Agumon Evolves From Koromon, among others. dmc-v1's Koromon carries four energies already; the Nature Spirits line's own Koromon had vitality free, so the snow Agumon sits beside that line's Agumon.

nodes.comment: Candmon's egg. Candmon is already a Rookie of this line (`petimeramon` -> `candmon`), so its egg roots the line that reaches it.

Betamon's egg. Betamon itself is one of the 157 idle-only Digimon and can never be on an edge, so this egg cannot be wired to its own species; it is placed where that species would go. Wikimon lists Betamon as evolving from Pukamon, and `pukamon` is this line's In-Training, so the Deep Savers line is the honest home.
Kamemon's egg, and the same case as beta_digitama: Kamemon is idle-only and cannot be on an edge. Wikimon lists Kamemon as evolving from Pukamon, this line's In-Training, so it hatches here.

Kudamon's egg. Kudamon is idle-only, so no thread can be built to it and no canonical baby of it is claimed here: Wikimon draws Kudamon from Bibimon and Kyaromon, neither of which this line holds. It is placed on the Virus Busters line because Kudamon is a Holy Beast of that family, which is a flavour call and is recorded as one.
Kudamon (2006)'s egg - the same Digimon under the Savers sheet, and idle-only too. Placed beside kuda_digitama by the variant rule.

Espimon's egg. Espimon has NO sheet at all in `16x16 Digimon Sprites` - not even an idle-only one - so the species is unreachable in this app and the egg can only be placed by where that species belongs. Wikimon lists Espimon as evolving from Caprimon, and `caprimon` is this line's In-Training, so Metal Empire is where it goes.

Guilmon's egg, and the root of the Tamers partners' line. Wikimon: Jyarimon evolves to Gigimon, and Guilmon evolves from Gigimon, so Jyarimon is Guilmon's own Baby I and this is the whole thread's bottom rung.

BlackGuilmon's egg. Wikimon records no Evolves From for BlackGuilmon at all, so the variant rule decides: it hangs off the base form's line and hatches onto the base form's Baby I. Two eggs on one Baby I is deliberate - a Baby I may have several in-edges, and each egg still has exactly one hatch edge.
Impmon's egg. Wikimon: Kiimon evolves to Yarmon (Yaamon), and Impmon evolves from Yarmon, so Kiimon is Impmon's own Baby I. Impmon is a Tamers partner, hence this line.

Blucomon's egg. Wikimon: Cocomon evolves to Chocomon, and Blucomon evolves from Chocomon. Chocomon is also Lopmon's In-Training, which is why Blucomon rides the Tamers thread's baby rungs rather than opening a line of its own - US-145's lop_digitama hatches onto this same Cocomon.
Guilmon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Gigimon, which US-147 wires - see the dead-end ledger in DigitamaSweepTests. US-144 authors the Digitama rung only.
Impmon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Yaamon, which US-147 wires.
The Baby I under Chocomon, which is Lopmon's and Blucomon's In-Training. A LEAF for now: US-147 wires Chocomon.

Gaomon's egg, and the root of the beast-cub line. Wikimon: Dodomon evolves to Wanyamon, and Gaomon evolves from Wanyamon. Dodomon is also Dorumon's Baby I, which costs nothing - a Baby I may carry several out-edges, so US-148 can hang Dorumon's thread off the same node.
Bearmon's egg. Wikimon: Popomon evolves to Wanyamon, and Bearmon evolves from Wanyamon. Popomon rather than Dodomon so the two cubs do not share a Baby I while their Baby II already does.
Koemon's egg. Wikimon: Koemon evolves from Wanyamon, which is what Popomon leads to. Beside bear_digitama on the same Baby I.
Gaomon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Wanyamon, which US-147 wires.
Bearmon's and Koemon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Wanyamon, which US-147 wires.

Keramon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Tsumemon, which US-147 wires.

Commandramon's Baby I. A LEAF for now, and the one whose canonical Baby II (Missimon) has NO sheet on disk - US-147 has to pick a stand-in rather than a straight lookup.

Lucemon's egg. Wikimon: Tsubumon evolves to Tokomon, and Lucemon evolves from Tokomon - and `tokomon` is already this line's Baby II, so `tsubumon` joins as a second Baby I of Ver.3 and US-147 can close the dead end with an edge to a node that exists.

Meicoomon's egg. Meicoomon's OWN Baby I, `meicoo_baby`, is one of the 157 idle-only Digimon, so this thread cannot be opened at the bottom however much the allocation would like it to be. Wikimon lists Keramon first among Meicoomon's Evolves From, and Keramon's Baby I here is `kuramon`, so the egg joins the network-virus line rather than inventing a rung.

Mushmon's egg. Mushmon is already a Child of this line, so by the sweep's first rule the egg roots the line that reaches it and adds no rung; Wikimon draws Mushmon from Nyokimon, which is this line's Baby I, so the arrow agrees with the placement.

PawnChessmon (White)'s egg, beside pawnchessblack_digitama by the variant rule and by the same Wikimon arrow: Budmon evolves to both colours, and Budmon evolves from Botamon.

Plotmon's egg. Plotmon is already a Child of this line, so the egg adds no rung; Wikimon lists Yukimi Botamon among Plotmon's Evolves From directly, and `yukimibotamon` is this line's Baby I.
Pulsemon's egg. Wikimon: Dokimon evolves to Bibimon, and Pulsemon evolves from Bibimon. Pulsemon is drawn on Vital Bracelet Digital Monster and Vital Bracelet BE, which is this line.

Swimmon's egg. Swimmon is already a Child of this line, so the egg adds no rung; Wikimon draws Swimmon from Pinamon, and Pinamon from Botamon, which is this line's Baby I.

Vorvomon's egg. Wikimon draws Vorvomon from Peti Meramon, and Peti Meramon from Mokumon - this line's Baby II and Baby I - so the egg joins Nightmare Soldiers beside picodevi_digitama rather than opening a thread for a species that is already reachable through no rung of its own.
Wormmon's egg. Wikimon: Leafmon evolves to Minomon, and Wormmon evolves from Minomon. Wormmon is Ken's Adventure 02 partner, beside V-mon.

Zubamon's egg. Lalamon's Baby I. A LEAF for now, but the shortest one in the file: its Baby II is Tanemon, which is ALREADY on this line, so US-147 closes this dead end with an edge rather than a node.
Lucemon's Baby I. A LEAF for now, and the same shape as `pipimon`: its Baby II is Tokomon, which is already this line's, so US-147 needs only the edge.

THE XROS LOADER LINE'S JUNK CHAMPION, and a FLAVOUR pick rather than a cited arrow: the line had no Adult rung at all, and EvolutionCriteriaTests requires every branching Child to fall to a junk Champion of its own line. Targetmon is a Xros Wars gag Digimon - a walking shooting-gallery target - which is what makes it read as junk, and it takes an orphan out of the pool instead of being an alias.

Xros Loader. Wikimon gives Starmon (2010 Anime Version) exactly one Evolves From, Pickmon, which is this line's Baby II. Its stranding is INHERITED and stated rather than hidden: all three xros Baby II are unreachable because the line has no Digitama and US-144/145 spent all 57 eggs - see EvolutionCriteriaTests.

Dorulumon is the Xros Wars cast's own wolf and Starmon rides with it in the same series, so it stands as the Champion of this line.
Xros Loader. Wikimon: Shoutmon Evolves To Shoutmon (King Ver.), which this pack files as an Adult sheet of its own.

REHOME. Wikimon: Meicoomon's Unnamed Child Form Evolves To Meicoomon, and Meicoomon's own Evolves From names it in return. One species over two rungs.

SandYanmamon keeps an insect Child on an insect Champion of its own line.

THE VITAL BRACELET LINE'S JUNK CHAMPION, and a FLAVOUR pick rather than a cited arrow: the line had no Adult rung at all, and EvolutionCriteriaTests requires every branching Child to fall to a junk Champion of its own line. Kokeshimon is a painted wooden doll with no limbs - a gag sheet, which is what makes it read as junk - and it takes an orphan out of the pool rather than being an alias.

FLAVOUR. Wikimon: BlackGabumon Evolves To Garurumon (Black), which is this sheet.

nodes.comment: Wikimon: Akatorimon Evolves From Floramon (Digimon World 2) and Evolves To Garudamon (Bo-262, Digimon Championship). Its junk edge is the tree's own - every Champion of `penc-wg` falls to TonosamaGekomon.

conditions.hint: Win far more often than it loses
And climb, so the wings learn height

nodes.comment: Wikimon: Black Gaogamon Evolves From Gaomon (Digimon Crusader) and Evolves To Black Mach Gaogamon (Digimon Crusader). The variant rule sends it to its base form's line, and Gaogamon is on `wanyamon` - the cited parent is on that line too, so no rehome was needed. `wanyamon` had NO Perfect rung at all before this story: BlackMachGaogamon opens it as the earned branch and Karakurumon as the junk floor under it.

conditions.hint: A black beast is proved in the ring
And in the hours it never stops moving

nodes.comment: The first Perfect this line has ever had. Wikimon draws it out of Black Gaogamon in Digimon Crusader, which is the only in-edge it is given here; it is a leaf until the Ultimate sweeps, exactly as US-149 and US-150 left their Champions.

The junk floor `wanyamon` needed before any Champion of the line could branch - the same problem US-148 and US-149 solved one rung down, now at the Perfect rung. Chosen off an unused sheet to follow Manekimon, this line's junk Champion: a beckoning-cat figurine falls to a clockwork puppet. No source document draws it - the whole-file grep over the tree markdown finds neither name.

Wikimon: Black Tailmon Evolves From Plotmon (St-176, DM-059) and Evolves To Holy Angemon (Pendulum Progress 2.0, Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode). Both are on `penc-vb`, where the variant rule also wants it: Tailmon is on this line as `pencvb_tailmon`, beside Tailmon X. Plotmon's vitality branch is the white Tailmon and this is its strength one - the same Rookie, raised to fight instead.

conditions.hint: Almost never lose
And drill the claw that does it

nodes.comment: NO CITATION IS CLAIMED FOR THE PARENT EDGE. Wikimon gives Burgamon Adult exactly three Evolves From - Burgamon, Ebi Burgamon and Tyumon - and not one of them can carry an edge: Ebi Burgamon has no sheet at all, Tyumon is idle-only, and Burgamon is on disk as `burgermon`, which is one of the 157 idle-only Digimon `edgeToDexOnlyNode` forbids. So the parent rung is unwireable rather than merely missing, and this is a rehome. It goes to `dmc-v4` because the file already puts this family there: Torikara Ballmon, whose only canon relatives ANYWHERE are the two Burgamon Adult, is a Baby II of this line, and Digitamamon - one of Burgamon Adult's two cited Evolves To (Bo-21L) - is its Perfect. Muchomon is the Child the file already draws above Torikara Ballmon, so it is the stand-in. The OUT-edge is cited; only the in-edge is flavour.

conditions.hint: Work every service to the last order
A shop is kept open on its feet

nodes.comment: The other half of the pair, and the same rehome for the same reason - see `burgermon_papa`, whose comment carries the whole argument. Wikimon's Frontier entry is explicit that there are TWO Burgamon Adult and that they are the parents of the Torikara Ballmon this line already holds, which is why they are two nodes off one Child rather than one node under two names. Papa is Muchomon's vitality branch and Mama its stamina one, so both are reachable.

conditions.hint: Carry every plate to every table
And never once take the weight off

nodes.comment: Wikimon: Dark Lizamon Evolves From Monodramon (St-632) and Evolves To Megalo Growmon (with Flare Lizamon, Digimon Story: Sunburst & Moonlight). Monodramon is on `tamers`, which is also where the anime puts Dark Lizamon - it is the wild Digimon Guilmon fights in Digimon Tamers. `tamers` had NO Perfect rung at all before this story, and it is the largest line in the file: MegaloGrowmon opens it as the earned branch and CatchMamemon as the junk floor under it, which is what the twenty-two other Champions of this line will need in US-152..US-156.

conditions.hint: Win the fights it picks
And burn everything it has in them

nodes.comment: The first Perfect this line has ever had. Wikimon draws it out of Dark Lizamon (with Flare Lizamon), and Flare Lizamon is US-152's - it is an E-G Champion still orphaned after this story, so only one of the two cited parents could be wired here. A leaf until the Ultimate sweeps.

The junk floor `tamers` needed before any Champion of the line could branch. Chosen off an unused sheet to follow Numemon X, this line's junk Champion: a crane-game cabinet is exactly what the franchise's prize-junk Digimon falls into. No source document draws it - the whole-file grep over the tree markdown finds neither name.

CLASS-LEVEL CITATIONS ONLY, and Wikimon has nothing else to give: the page's entire Evolves From is "Any Blue Lv.3 Digimon from the Digimon Card Game" and its Evolves To names no Perfect on disk - Decker Greymon has no sheet, and Mugendramon is an Ultimate, a rung too far. What the Evolves To DOES give is "Any Digimon that also Evolves from Cyborg Lv.4 Digimon", and Andromon is reached here from Guardromon, this line's Cyborg Champion, so the out-edge satisfies the clause literally. `penc-me` is the machine line and Deckerdramon is a Cyborg Digimon; the Hagurumon parent is the flavour half, and no named citation is claimed for it.

conditions.hint: A tank is measured in ground covered
And in the guns it has actually fired

nodes.comment: Wikimon: Ebidramon Evolves From Shakomon (bolded, the arrow the page treats as primary) and Evolves To Anomalocarimon (with certain Digimon of the Pendulum series, or any Data Adult Digimon from the Digimon Pendulum COLOR, cited to Pendulum COLOR 2 Deep Savers). Both ends are already on `penc-ds`: Shakomon is the Deep Savers Rookie US-139 wired and Anomalocarimon is the Perfect over its Seadramon branch, so the line follows from the citation rather than from flavour. Vitality is the energy Shakomon had left.

conditions.hint: Carry it the length of a trench
And let the claws win more than they lose
Walk it along the deep shelf
And let the old shell learn to fight
Crawl the trench end to end
And harden the shell on something that bites
Take it out past the shelf and back
And let the harpoon settle every argument

Kamemon is the bolded parent and would have been first choice, but `kamemon` is idle-only in this pack and `edgeToDexOnlyNode` forbids an edge to an idle-only Digimon - the same trap US-151 hit with Burgamon. Gomamon is the next cited parent and is this line's own Rookie, so no rehome was needed and the citation survives intact. Stamina is the energy Gomamon had left.

conditions.hint: A DJ works a long set on its feet
And never lets the tempo drop

nodes.comment: The other half of the pairing US-151 could only wire one end of. Wikimon: Flare Lizamon Evolves To Megalo Growmon (with Dark Lizamon), and DarkLizamon has been pointing at MegaloGrowmon from this line since US-151 - so the arrow the file already draws is completed rather than a second one invented. The in-edge is cited too: Clear Agumon is in Flare Lizamon's Evolves From, and it is on `tamers`. Strength is the energy ClearAgumon had left.

conditions.hint: Keep the mane burning past exhaustion
And win the fights it starts

nodes.comment: THE VARIANT RULE, and it needed no rehome: Wikimon draws Growmon (Orange) out of Guilmon and into Megalo Growmon, which is the plain Growmon's own thread and is already on `tamers`. So the variant sits with its base form under the same parent, and both of its edges are cited. Vitality is the energy Guilmon had left after Growmon took strength.

conditions.hint: Wear the claws down on open ground
And drill it until the blades come out clean

And drill the arm blades past the point of aching

nodes.comment: Wikimon: Gulus Gammamon Evolves From Gammamon (bolded, and cited to Pendulum COLOR ZERO Virus Busters among others) and Evolves To Holy Angemon (with any Data or Vaccine Adult Digimon from the Digimon Pendulum COLOR). Both are on `penc-vb` already - Gammamon since US-149, HolyAngemon since US-143 - so this is Gammamon's dark second branch beside BetelGammamon, which is exactly what the pair is in Ghost Game. Strength is the energy Gammamon had left.

conditions.hint: A thing of the dark needs its long nights
And has to win its way back to the light
Let the little heart dream under the whole sky
And keep the frame lean enough for the stars

nodes.comment: Wikimon: Kaus Gammamon Evolves From Gammamon (bolded) and Evolves To Were Garurumon (with any Data Adult Digimon from the Digimon Pendulum COLOR), and BOTH of those arrows are cited to Pendulum COLOR ZERO Virus Busters - the device this line is named for. Its family field says Virus Busters outright. Both ends were already on `penc-vb`, Gammamon since US-149 and WereGarurumon since US-143, so no Perfect was spent and no line argument had to be made. The bolded Evolves To is Canoweissmon, which has a Perfect sheet but no node; taking WereGarurumon instead keeps the story at three nodes and leaves Canoweissmon to the Perfect sweeps. Stamina is the energy Gammamon had left after BetelGammamon took spirit and GulusGammamon strength, and it suits the glider: Wikimon's profile has it soaring on its arm membranes and its knee jets.

conditions.hint: Let it glide the whole length of a valley
And let the cool head win more than it loses

nodes.comment: US-152's rule applied before the line was chosen: intersect BOTH ends against the graph. Wikimon draws Kinkakumon out of Jellymon and into Zudomon, both cited to Vital Bracelet BE, and both are already on `penc-ds` - Jellymon is the tree's unlockable sixth Rookie and Zudomon is Ikkakumon's Perfect - so this costs no new Perfect. The Saiyu Warriors reading was the other candidate and was rejected on price rather than on citation: Fujamon and Takinmon are cited parents too, but `penc-sw` has NO Perfect rung at all, so it would have cost an earned Perfect plus a junk floor. Its Perfect rung is left to the Perfect sweeps. Strength is the energy Jellymon had left after TeslaJellymon took spirit, and the profile earns it: an Oni that swings a Seven-Star Gold Rod and kicks lightning.

conditions.hint: Keep the rod arm working until it aches
And send it headlong into fight after fight

nodes.comment: Wikimon: Kougamon Evolves From Mushmon (Digimon Story: Sunburst & Moonlight, and D-alpha-508) and Evolves To Jyureimon (D-alpha-508, with or without Igamon), and both are on `penc-wg` already - Mushmon is the tree's Mushroomon and Jyureimon is the Perfect over Woodmon. The same page notes that D-alpha-508 has Kougamon treated as a Togemon when evolving, which is what puts a ninja monkey on a plant line rather than flavour. Spirit is the energy Mushmon had left after Woodmon took vitality and RedVegimon strength, and it suits the Kouga-style shadow clones the profile gives it. Kougamon has an idle-only sheet in `Idle Frame Only/` as well; the 48x64 one in `Adult/` is what the roster entry points at.

conditions.hint: Move it shadow to shadow across the whole map
And practise the clone trick until it splits clean

nodes.comment: US-152's rule first: intersect BOTH ends against the graph before choosing a line. Wikimon draws Meramon (X-Antibody) out of Blucomon and into Grademon, and BOTH arrows are cited to Digital Monster X Ver.3 - the device that gave this file every X-Antibody Rookie it has, all of which sit on `tamers`. The other cited parents are DORUmon and Terriermon (X-Antibody), also `tamers`, so no reading of this page puts it anywhere else. Its three other cited Evolves To - Cyberdramon (X-Antibody), Mamemon (X-Antibody) and Omega Shoutmon (X-Antibody) - all have Perfect sheets on disk and no node, and are left to the Perfect sweeps; Grademon was chosen because Pegasmon X cites the same Perfect off the same device, so one new node serves two Champions. Strength is the energy Blucomon had left after PaleDramon took vitality, and a walking pillar of flame earns it.

conditions.hint: Let the fire burn hot enough to hurt
And walk it into fight after fight without cooling

nodes.comment: The one Champion in this range whose two ends could NOT both be named, and the honest reading is written here rather than dressed up. Wikimon's Evolves From cites Plotmon outright (Digimon Masters, Digimon Soul Chaser: "Evolves from Plotmon at level 11"), and Plotmon is this line's Salamon, so the in-edge is a plain citation. Every named Evolves To is unusable in this pack: Bastemon and Betsumon have no sheet at all, Majiramon is idle-only and `edgeToDexOnlyNode` forbids the edge, and Zudomon is on `penc-ds` where no cited parent of Mikemon lives. What is left is the CLASS citation the same page carries - Mikemon is entered from "Any Yellow Lv.3 Digimon from the Digimon Card Game" (BT12-036) and leaves to "Any Yellow Lv.5 Digimon" - and Plotmon is a Yellow Lv.3 and Angewomon a Yellow Lv.5, so both ends of the class clause are satisfied by one line. That is the Deckerdramon reading US-151 recorded: a class-level Wikimon citation is a real citation. Stamina is the last energy Plotmon had, after Tailmon took vitality, Wizarmon spirit and BlackTailmon strength - Plotmon is FULL now.

conditions.hint: Keep the cat on its feet instead of curled up
And drill the claws until they land clean

nodes.comment: Both arrows off one device. Wikimon draws Monochromon (X-Antibody) out of Guilmon (X-Antibody) (Sx-45, Pendulum X 2.0 and 2.5) and into Mametyramon (Bx-43, "Jogress with or without Pteranomon or Pteranomon (X-Antibody)"), and Guilmon X has been this line's since US-149. Mametyramon is the ONE Perfect this story authored twice over: Pteranomon X's own page cites the same card from the other side, so the two Champions Bx-43 fuses are the two parents of the node. The base Monochromon is on `dmc-v4` and stays there - the variant follows its X-Antibody Rookie, which is the rule the whole `tamers` X thread was built on. Strength is the energy Guilmon X had left after Allomon X took stamina.

conditions.hint: Push the armoured bulk uphill until it stops complaining
And let the charge win more often than it misses

nodes.comment: Both ends cited to the SAME source, which is as clean as this rung gets: Wikimon has Mori Shellmon evolving from Ganimon and to Anomalocarimon, both to Digimon World 2, and both were already on `penc-ds` - Ganimon is the tree's third Rookie and Anomalocarimon the Perfect over Seadramon and Ebidramon. Its other cited parents are Betamon (idle-only here), Dokunemon (`palmon`) and Palmon; the Digimon World 2 reading is the only one whose other end was already on the same line, which is why it decided the placement. Spirit is the energy Ganimon had left after Coelamon took stamina and Gesomon vitality, and a shell with a forest growing out of its back earns the quiet one.

conditions.hint: Let it lie still long enough for the moss to take
And still make it heave that shell around

nodes.comment: Wikimon gives Musyamon twenty-odd cited parents and as many children; the pair that matters is the one whose BOTH ends were already on a line - Pico Devimon below (Digimon Story: Sunburst & Moonlight) and Vamdemon above (Bo-214), and this tree holds both. Its own family field reads Virus / Demon Man, which is what Nightmare Soldiers is, so the cheap reading and the thematic one agree here for once. Kotemon, Goburimon, Shamamon and Zubamon are the parents Wikimon bolds hardest and NONE of them is playable in this pack - Shamamon and Zubamon are idle-only, the other two have no sheet - which is why the choice fell to Pico Devimon rather than to a rehome. Strength is the only energy Pico Devimon had left after Devimon took spirit, Bakemon vitality and Dokugumon stamina; it shares that energy with the junk fallback to Gokimon, which is the Scumon arrangement US-133 recorded - the earned edge wins whenever its own 120 gate is cleared.

conditions.hint: Drill the sword arm until the cut is one motion
And test it on something that fights back
Keep the coffin shut through every dawn
And let the count take what it wants by night

nodes.comment: Wikimon draws Nefertimon (X-Antibody) out of Plotmon (X-Antibody) (Bx-98 and Digital Monster X Ver.2) and into Angewomon (St-445), and both were already on `penc-vb` - Plotmon X since US-150 and Angewomon since US-143. Nefertimon is Plotmon's own armour form, so the variant sits over the variant Rookie rather than over the base, and the holy end of this tree is where an Armor Digimon of the Angewomon thread belongs. Herissmon is the other cited parent on this same line and was left free. Spirit is the energy Plotmon X had after Tailmon X took vitality.

conditions.hint: Work it in the open until the stone wings warm
And let the holy beast win the fights it takes

nodes.comment: Wikimon gives Pegasmon (X-Antibody) exactly four cited parents and four cited children, all to Digital Monster X Ver.3: in from Jazamon, Lopmon (X-Antibody) and Ryudamon, out to Angewomon (X-Antibody), Grademon, Hisyaryumon and Monzaemon (X-Antibody). Ryudamon is on `commandramon`, which is stranded and has no Perfect rung; Jazamon and Lopmon X are both `tamers`, so that is the line. Grademon is the one of the four cited Perfects Meramon X ALSO cites off the same device, which is why one new node covers both Champions and the other three - all of them sheets on disk with no node - are left to the Perfect sweeps. Vitality is the energy Lopmon X had left after BlackGalgomon took spirit.

conditions.hint: Let the wings carry it a very long way
And school the gallop until it lands square

nodes.comment: The other half of Bx-43. Wikimon draws Pteranomon (X-Antibody) out of Monodramon (Bx-42, Da-484) and into Mametyramon ("with Monochromon or Monochromon (X-Antibody)", Bx-43) - the same card Monochromon X's page cites from the other side, so the two Champions the card fuses are the two parents this story gives the node. Its bolder Evolves To, Andromon, is on `dmc-v3`, and Patamon - the only cited parent on that line - carries all five edges it can hold, so the dmc-v3 reading was closed before it was priced. Vitality is the energy Monodramon had left after Coredramon Blue took strength and DarkLizamon spirit.

conditions.hint: Keep it in the air over open ground
And make it climb for every thermal it rides

nodes.comment: The variant on its base form's line, and both ends cited. Wikimon draws Red V-dramon out of Piyomon (Digimon World -next 0rder-) and into Aero V-dramon (Digimon Adventure 02: D1 Tamers, Digital Monster: D-Project), and `penc-wg` already holds all three of Piyomon, V-dramon and AeroV-dramon - V-dramon is Piyomon's strength branch and AeroV-dramon the Perfect above it, so the red one hangs beside its own base form rather than across the file from it. The Guilmon and Shoutmon readings were rejected on the same test US-153 used: both are cited too, but `tamers` and `xros` would each have cost more, and neither holds AeroV-dramon. Stamina is the energy Piyomon had left after Birdramon took vitality and V-dramon strength.

conditions.hint: Keep the wings beating long past comfortable
And blood the V-Breath on real opponents

nodes.comment: The second earned Perfect this line has ever had, and it exists because TWO of this story's Champions cite it off the same device: Wikimon lists Grademon in the Evolves To of both Meramon (X-Antibody) and Pegasmon (X-Antibody), each to Digital Monster X Ver.3. Opening it cost one node rather than two, because US-151 already put CatchMamemon under this line as its junk floor. A leaf until the Ultimate sweeps.

The third earned Perfect on this line, and like Grademon it serves two of this story's Champions at once - but here the two parents are the card's own Jogress pair. Wikimon's Bx-43 draws Mametyramon out of Monochromon (X-Antibody) "with or without Pteranomon or Pteranomon (X-Antibody)", and Pteranomon X's page cites the same card from its side, so both arrows are one drawing. A leaf until the Ultimate sweeps.

Wikimon gives Saberdramon sixteen cited parents and twenty-odd cited children, and exactly one line already held a node at BOTH ends - `dmc-v4`, where Muchomon (Da-511) is a Rookie and Megadramon (Digimon World -next 0rder-, and its International Edition) is the Perfect over Airdramon. That is the US-152 rule: intersect Evolves From AND Evolves To before choosing the line, rather than choosing off the parent and paying for a new Perfect afterwards. The other pairings were priced and rejected: Pico Devimon and Candmon reach Vamdemon and Pumpmon on `penc-nso`, but Pico Devimon is full as of US-154 and Candmon's arrow is the DSSM Jogress with Dot Falcomon, which has no sheet here; Piyomon reaches Garudamon on `penc-wg` but US-154 spent Piyomon's last energy on RedV-dramon. Spirit is the energy Muchomon had left after Kuwagamon took strength and the two Burgermon vitality and stamina, and a bird of black flame that hunts at night is the quiet, dark one of the four.

conditions.hint: Take it higher than a thing that heavy should go
And let a temper that ferocious find its fights

nodes.comment: Both arrows to the SAME source, which is the cleanest a placement gets: Wikimon draws Shima Unimon out of Bakumon and into Mammon, each cited to Digimon World 2, and this tree already held both - Bakumon since US-140 and Mammon as the Perfect over Mojyamon's thread. Its other readings were real but dearer: Elecmon and Elecmon Violet reach Skull Greymon on `dmc-v2`, Yuki Agumon reaches Asuramon on `penc-nsp`, and Clear Agumon, Gaomon and Kokuwamon each reach a Perfect on a line their own does not hold. Spirit is the energy Bakumon had left after Hanumon took strength, Garurumon vitality and Loogarmon stamina, and a striped Unimon that lost the Pegasus wings and kept the unicorn horn is the one of the four that is more idea than muscle. This fills Bakumon: four earned branches, one per energy, plus the junk fallback.

conditions.hint: Work the legs that gave up flying for speed
And let it rest long enough to grow into tusks
March it across the whole of the frozen plain
And let it carry weight it grew, not weight you gave

nodes.comment: Wikimon gives Siesamon (X-Antibody) four cited parents and four cited children, every one of them to Digital Monster X Ver.3: in from Blucomon, DORUmon and Terriermon (X-Antibody), out to DORUguremon, Garudamon (X-Antibody), Grademon and Omega Shoutmon (X-Antibody). All three parents are already on `tamers`, and of the four children exactly one is a node - Grademon, which US-154 opened for Meramon X and Pegasmon X off this same device. So the pairing costs nothing at the rung above, and DORUmon is the parent taken because DORUguremon, its own Perfect, is the other arrow this page draws. The base Siesamon is on `algomon` and stays there: the variant follows the X thread, which is where every X-Antibody Digimon in this file lives. Strength is the energy DORUmon had left beside Dorugamon's spirit.

conditions.hint: Spend the sunlight it charges rather than hoarding it
And let the holy beast win the calamities it meets

nodes.comment: The oldest arrow in the franchise, and both halves of it are cited to the same device: Wikimon bolds Agumon at the head of Tyranomon's Evolves From to Digital Monster Ver. 1, and names Mamemon in its Evolves To to Digital Monster Ver. 1 as well. `dmc-v1` already held both, so the rung above cost nothing. Its Evolves To is one of the longest on the wiki and several other entries are nodes here - Megadramon on `dmc-v4`, Metal Tyranomon and Ex-Tyranomon on `dmc-v5`, Mametyramon on `tamers` - but every one of them is on a line no cited parent of Tyranomon reaches, and `testNoEdgeCrossesALine` is what makes that decide rather than flavour. Vitality is the last energy Agumon had, after Greymon took strength, Meramon stamina and Devimon spirit; a dinosaur famous for being docile and easy to raise is the one that comes of plain good health. This fills Agumon.

conditions.hint: Keep the heavy one on its feet, hour after hour
And drill the docile one until it is anything but

nodes.comment: The X mirror of the arrow above it, and cited the same way twice: Wikimon draws Tyranomon (X-Antibody) out of Agumon (X-Antibody) to Digital Monster X and into Metal Greymon (X-Antibody) to Digital Monster X. Agumon X is on `dmc-v3`, so that is the line, and Metal Greymon X had to be opened because NONE of the other cited children could be reached from it - Mametyramon is on `tamers`, Metal Tyranomon and Ex-Tyranomon on `dmc-v5`, and Dorbickmon, Metal Tyranomon X and Yatagaramon 2006 have no node. That cost one node rather than two, because `dmc-v3` already has Etemon as its junk floor. The other cited parents were rejected on the same test: Herissmon is on `penc-vb`, which holds none of the children; Dark Tyranomon is a Champion, not a Rookie; and Tyranomon itself is this story's own Champion on `dmc-v1`. Vitality is the energy Agumon X had beside Greymon X's strength, and it is the same energy the base Agumon spends on the base Tyranomon one line over.

conditions.hint: Keep it rolling until the ridge on its back cuts air
And make it fight its way through everything that comes

nodes.comment: The one Perfect this story had to open, and it exists because every OTHER Perfect Wikimon names in Tyranomon (X-Antibody)'s Evolves To is either on a line `dmc-v3` cannot reach or has no sheet at all. Metal Greymon (X-Antibody) is cited to Digital Monster X, the same device that draws Agumon X into Tyranomon X, so the whole thread is one drawing. It cost one node rather than two: US-133 put Etemon under this line as its junk floor long before this story. Wikimon also draws it from Greymon (X-Antibody), which is already a Champion here and a dead end - that arrow is left to the Perfect sweeps rather than drawn now, so this node has exactly one parent. A leaf until the Ultimate sweeps.

Wikimon gives V-dramon (Black) exactly two cited parents - Guilmon and Guilmon (X-Antibody), both to Bo-552 - and both are on `tamers`, which holds not one of its cited children. So the US-152 intersection is EMPTY on the parent's line, and taking it would have bought a Perfect on a line with no other claim on this Digimon. The other end is the opposite: Aero V-dramon is cited eight times over (St-730, Bo-88, Bo-216, Bo-476, Bo-935, Bo-1113, Bo-2v, Vj-7), and Bo-1113 draws it from V-dramon (Black) with or without XV-mon (Black), so the arrow stands alone. AeroV-dramon is already a Perfect on `penc-wg`, which is where the base V-dramon and RedV-dramon both live under this same Rookie - the criteria's own rule that a variant hangs off its base form's line, and the reading that costs nothing one rung up. The in-edge is therefore a LINE argument rather than a citation, and it is said here rather than dressed up: no source draws Piyomon into V-dramon (Black). Spirit is the last energy Pyocomon's Piyomon had, after Birdramon took vitality, V-dramon strength and RedV-dramon stamina, and the black one of three dragons is the one that comes of a quiet, dark-hours upbringing. This fills `pencwg_piyomon`.

conditions.hint: Climb until the ground stops being the point
And let the black dragon settle things the way a dragon does

nodes.comment: Both ends cited to a source that names a node already on one line, which is the whole US-152 rule in one Digimon. Wikimon draws XV-mon (Black) from Flamon, Mushmon and V-mon, all three to St-768; Flamon has no sheet in this pack and V-mon is on `adventure02`, which has no Perfect rung at all and would have cost two nodes, while Mushmon is on `penc-wg` - where AeroV-dramon, cited in this same page's Evolves To to Bo-935 and drawn by Bo-1113 with or without V-dramon (Black), is already the Perfect. So the variant followed its cited Rookie rather than its base form, which is US-155's Tyranomon X reading a second time: the base XV-mon stays where US-150 left it on `adventure02` and is deliberately not moved. Stamina is the energy Mushmon had left after Woodmon took vitality, RedVegimon strength and US-153's Kougamon spirit, and the armoured one of the four is the one that comes of endurance. This fills Mushmon.

conditions.hint: Keep it moving long past the point a dragon would land
And drill it until the V-Laser is one motion, not two
Run the two halves together until they keep step
And let the pair win far more than they lose
Take the three-legged crow above the shrine
And teach it the road before it flies it

nodes.comment: The handover US-153 wrote and US-154 and US-155 both passed on, taken up and then CORRECTED. The advice was that Gammamon - which Wikimon bolds at the head of Wezen Gammamon's Evolves From, citing Ghost Game 12 'Chain Letter', Vital Bracelet Digital Monster, Vital Bracelet BE, BT10-050 and Pendulum COLOR ZERO Virus Busters - was FULL, so this Champion had to take the Gabumon citation on the same line instead. It is not full. Gammamon carries BetelGammamon on spirit, GulusGammamon on strength and KausGammamon on stamina, and its junk edge to Turuiemon shares spirit with the first, so VITALITY was free the whole time and the three-earned-branch reading US-153 wrote down was one branch short of the four-energy ceiling. So the bolded arrow is drawn after all, and Gabumon keeps its vitality for a later story. The out-edge is Canoweissmon, bolded in Evolves To and cited to the same Pendulum COLOR ZERO Virus Busters plus Vital Bracelet Digital Monster, BT10-011, RB1-009 and Ghost Game 26 - drawn with OR WITHOUT any Vaccine Adult, so it is an evolution and not only a fusion, and the whole Ghost Game thread Gammamon -> Wezen Gammamon -> Canoweissmon is one drawing. Metal Greymon was rejected precisely there: it is already a Perfect on this line and would have cost nothing at all, but Wikimon draws it only WITH any Data Adult Digimon, a strict Jogress, and this file does not spend Jogress arrows as edges. Regulusmon has a sheet and no node, so it cost the same one node as Canoweissmon while being cited less; Bombermon, Dorbickmon, Fumamon, Ghilliedhumon, Tankdramon and Zanmetsumon have no sheet at all. This fills Gammamon for good.

conditions.hint: Walk it until the white knight is the last thing standing
And never let it gorge; Canoweissmon is all edge and no weight

nodes.comment: One game draws both arrows: Wikimon cites Piyomon -> Xiquemon to Digimon New Century, and Huankunmon - bolded - to Digimon New Century as well as Vital Bracelet BE and Pendulum COLOR 6 Saiyu Warriors. `dmc-v4` is Piyomon's own line and had spirit free, so the bill is this Champion plus one Perfect. THE REJECTED READING IS THE BETTER THEME AND IS WRITTEN DOWN HERE ON PURPOSE, as US-153's Kinkakumon was: Fujamon and Kamemon are both cited to Pendulum COLOR 6 Saiyu Warriors, which is literally the line `penc-sw`, and Huankunmon, Sagomon and Sanzomon are that same device's Perfects - the whole Journey to the West thread under one roof. It lost on cost. `penc-sw` has NO Perfect rung at all, so that reading is three nodes (this Champion, an earned Perfect and a junk floor beneath every Champion of the line) and a new `EvolutionCriteriaTests.junkIds` entry, against two here. Kamemon is idle-only in this pack in any case, and so is Falcomon 2006, so neither could have carried the in-edge; Luxmon has no sheet. When a later story opens `penc-sw`'s Perfect rung, Xiquemon is the first rehome candidate on it. Spirit is the last energy Piyomon had, after Leomon took strength, Monochromon vitality and Kuwagamon stamina, and a pilgrim bird is the quiet one of the four. This fills the `dmc-v4` seed Rookie.

conditions.hint: Let it dream the long dream a pilgrimage is made of
And drill the staff work until the road ahead is safe

nodes.comment: Wikimon bolds Renamon in Youkomon's Evolves From and cites it nine ways over (St-421, St-989, Bo-507, Da-507, Vital Bracelet Digital Monster, Digimon Championship, Digimon New Century, EX4-026 and Digimon Super Rumble), and it is on `tamers` with three energies still free, so the in-edge cost nothing. The out-edge is the expensive half and the reason is the one worth recording: BOTH of Youkomon's bolded children are idle-only in this pack - Doumon and Taomon exist in `Idle Frame Only/` alone, and `edgeToDexOnlyNode` forbids an edge to either - so the canonical fox thread cannot be drawn at all. Of what remains, BlackRapidmon is cited to Digimon Championship, the same game that draws Alraumon, Floramon and Psychemon into Youkomon, and it is the black Perfect of the Terriermon family that `tamers` already carries three ways as Terriermon, Terriermon X and Galgomon - so the new node lands on a line with a claim on it beyond this single arrow, and Galgomon is the obvious second parent for whichever Perfect sweep reaches it. Rejected: Atlur Kabuterimon (Blue) is on `penc-nsp` and Death Meramon on `penc-nso`, and no cited parent of Youkomon reaches either line, so `testNoEdgeCrossesALine` rules both out; LadyDevimon, Caturamon, Karatenmon and SkullBaluchimon have sheets but no node and no better citation than BlackRapidmon's. Stamina is what Renamon had free beside Kyubimon's spirit, and a courier fox with nine tails is the one that comes of running.

conditions.hint: Keep the fox running long after the tails have caught up
And let it finish its arguments in a single pass

nodes.comment: Wezen Gammamon's own bolded Evolves To on Wikimon, cited to Pendulum COLOR ZERO Virus Busters - the device this line is - as well as Vital Bracelet Digital Monster, BT10-011, RB1-009 and Ghost Game 26 'Cannibal Mansion', and drawn WITH OR WITHOUT any Vaccine Adult, so the solo arrow is the source's own. Opened for exactly one Champion. Wikimon also draws it straight out of Gammamon, which US-156 has just filled, so that second arrow can never be drawn and this node has one parent for good rather than for now. A leaf until the Ultimate sweeps, and on the dead-end ledger in `ChildSweepAToFTests` until one of them claims it.

Xiquemon's bolded Evolves To on Wikimon, cited to Digimon New Century - the same game that draws Piyomon into Xiquemon, so both halves of the thread are one drawing - and to Vital Bracelet BE and Pendulum COLOR 6 Saiyu Warriors besides. It sits on `dmc-v4` because its only parent here does, not because the Journey to the West cast belongs on Piyomon's device; `penc-sw` is where the theme points and where a later story may rehome the pair once that line has a Perfect rung, which is argued in full on the Xiquemon node. Sagomon and Sanzomon, the other two Perfects the same page names, are drawn only WITH a partner Adult and are left to a Jogress recipe rather than spent as edges. A leaf until the Ultimate sweeps.

Opened for Youkomon because Wikimon's two bolded alternatives, Doumon and Taomon, are both idle-only in this pack and cannot take an edge. It is cited from Youkomon to Digimon Championship, and it belongs on `tamers` twice over: that is the line Renamon is on, and it is also the line that already carries Terriermon, Terriermon X and Galgomon, whose Perfect BlackRapidmon is. Galgomon is deliberately NOT drawn into it here - Galgomon is a dead end on the ledger and the Perfect sweeps own it - so this node has exactly one parent for now. It cost one node rather than two because US-151 put CatchMamemon under this line as its junk floor. A leaf until the Ultimate sweeps.

Wikimon draws Andiramon out of Turuiemon - bolded, and cited both with and without a Virus Adult from the Vital Bracelet Digital Monster - and into Cherubimon (Vice), also bolded. BOTH ENDS WERE ALREADY ON `penc-vb`: Turuiemon has been this line's junk Champion since US-143 and Cherubimon (Vice) the Ultimate over Andiramon (Virus), so US-152's rule of intersecting `Evolves From` against `Evolves To` closes with no new node at all. Wendimon is the page's other bolded parent and has no sheet in this pack, which is why the arrow rests on Turuiemon. The Data and Virus Andiramon are now siblings on one line, the earned one and the junk one, which is exactly the shape their attributes describe. Vitality is the energy Turuiemon had free after Asuramon took strength.

Wikimon's bolded `Evolves From` for Angewomon (X-Antibody) is Tailmon (X-Antibody), and Tailmon X has sat on `penc-vb` as a LEAF since US-150 - so this arrow costs nothing below and clears a dead end at the same time. The climb is Ofanimon (X-Antibody), also on the page, which had no node: the X counterpart of the Ophanimon this line already puts over the plain Angewomon, so the pair reads as one family rather than two. Jazardmon and Pegasmon X are the page's other parents; both are `tamers`, which has no Angel rung, and the criteria's own rule puts a variant on its BASE FORM's line in any case. Spirit is what a holy cat earns and Tailmon X had every energy free.

NO CITATION FOR EITHER ARROW, AND THIS SAYS SO RATHER THAN DRESSING A LINE ARGUMENT AS ONE. Wikimon gives Anomalocarimon (X-Antibody) exactly one `Evolves From` - Anomalocarimon itself, with the X-Antibody - which is a Perfect and cannot be an in-edge here, and one `Evolves To`, Cherubimon (Virtue) (X-Antibody) [Digital Monster X Ver.3], which has no sheet in this pack. Both ends are therefore LINE arguments off the base form: Ebidramon is one of the three Champions that already reach the plain Anomalocarimon on `penc-ds`, and Aegisdramon is the Ultimate that Anomalocarimon already climbs into. The day a Cherubimon (Virtue) X sheet appears, the out-edge is the first thing to revisit. Stamina is the energy Ebidramon had free after vitality went to the base form.

Both arrows are Wikimon's and both land on `penc-nso`, which is what chose the line: Dokugumon is Archnemon's most heavily cited parent by a distance (Digimon Story, Sunburst & Moonlight, RPG, Masters, Lost Evolution and more) and has been this line's spider Champion since US-140, and Piemon - cited as 'Piemon (with Infermon or Fantomon)' - is already the Ultimate over Phantomon here.
